payload: {"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which form is correct? 'I ___ to school every day.'","options":["go","goes","going","gone"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"Simple Present with 'I' uses the base verb: 'go'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which form is correct? 'She ___ apples.'","options":["like","likes","liking","liked"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Simple Present 3rd person singular adds -s: 'she likes'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which form is correct? 'Look! The cat ___ the bird.'","options":["watches","watching","is watching","watch"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"'Look!' signals something happening NOW → Present Progressive: 'is watching'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which form is correct? 'I usually ___ tea in the morning.'","options":["am drinking","drink","drinks","drinking"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"'Usually' = habit → Simple Present: 'I drink'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose the right tense: 'Right now, the children ___ in the garden.'","options":["play","plays","are playing","played"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"'Right now' → Present Progressive: 'are playing'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which is correct? 'Every Sunday, my family ___ pizza.'","options":["eats","is eating","eating","eat"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"'Every Sunday' = habit → Simple Present 3rd person sg: 'eats'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"He ___ football. (play)","correctAnswer":"plays","hint":"3rd person singular adds -s","explanation":"'He plays' — Simple Present 3rd person sg."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"We ___ in Berlin. (live)","correctAnswer":"live","hint":"Plural subject — base form","explanation":"'We live' — no -s with plural subjects."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"Look! It ___ outside. (rain — Present Progressive)","correctAnswer":"is raining","hint":"Form: am/is/are + -ing","explanation":"Present Progressive: 'is raining'."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"My brother ___ TV every evening. (watch)","correctAnswer":"watches","hint":"3rd person sg, ends -ches","explanation":"'Watches' — verbs ending in -ch add -es."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"At the moment, my parents ___ dinner. (cook — Present Progressive)","correctAnswer":"are cooking","hint":"are + cooking","explanation":"Present Progressive with plural subject: 'are cooking'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"Sarah always ___ to bed at 9 pm. (go)","correctAnswer":"goes","hint":"'Always' = habit, 3rd person sg","explanation":"'Goes' — habit + 3rd person sg."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"With 'I' and 'you', we use the base form of the verb in Simple Present.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: I play, you play (no -s)."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"In Simple Present 3rd person singular (he/she/it), the verb adds -ing.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — it adds -s or -es: he plays, she watches, it goes."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Present Progressive describes actions happening right now.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: 'I am reading' = right now."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Words like 'every day', 'always', 'usually' signal Present Progressive.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — these signal Simple Present (habits)."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Right now' and 'at the moment' signal Present Progressive.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct — both indicate actions happening now."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Verbs ending in -ch or -sh add -s in 3rd person singular Simple Present.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — they add -es: watches, washes, brushes."}
{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["football","play","I"],"correctOrder":["I","play","football"],"explanation":"Subject + Verb + Object."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["likes","She","music"],"correctOrder":["She","likes","music"],"explanation":"Subject + Verb (3rd sg) + Object."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order (Present Progressive).","scrambledItems":["TV","is","watching","He"],"correctOrder":["He","is","watching","TV"],"explanation":"Subject + 'is' + verb-ing + Object."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["the","park","in","playing","are","children","The"],"correctOrder":["The","children","are","playing","in","the","park"],"explanation":"Subject + are + verb-ing + Place."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["always","tea","drinks","Mary","morning","in","the"],"correctOrder":["Mary","always","drinks","tea","in","the","morning"],"explanation":"Subject + Adverb of frequency + Verb + Object + Time."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["are","moment","at","reading","they","the"],"correctOrder":["At","the","moment","they","are","reading"],"explanation":"Time phrase + Subject + Present Progressive."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three sentences about what you do every day. Use Simple Present.","sampleAnswer":"I go to school. I eat lunch. I play with my friends.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Simple Present","Daily activities"],"explanation":"Simple Present for habits: I + base verb."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three sentences about your family using Simple Present.","sampleAnswer":"My father works in an office. My mother cooks dinner. My brother plays football.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Simple Present","3rd person sg with -s"],"explanation":"3rd person singular adds -s: works, cooks, plays."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Look outside. Write three sentences about what is happening NOW (Present Progressive).","sampleAnswer":"It is raining. The trees are moving in the wind. A bird is sitting on the roof.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Present Progressive (am/is/are + -ing)","Things happening now"],"explanation":"Present Progressive: be + verb-ing."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three sentences with 'always', 'usually', and 'sometimes'.","sampleAnswer":"I always brush my teeth in the morning. I usually walk to school. I sometimes read a book before bed.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Each with a frequency adverb","Simple Present"],"explanation":"Frequency adverbs go before the main verb."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write a short text (3 sentences) — mix Simple Present and Present Progressive.","sampleAnswer":"My family usually eats dinner at 6 pm. Today, however, my mother is making a special meal. We are celebrating my birthday.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Both tenses used","Logical context for each"],"explanation":"Simple Present for habits, Present Progressive for actions now or temporary situations."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Describe what you and your friends are doing at this moment (3 sentences, Present Progressive).","sampleAnswer":"My friend Tom is playing video games right now. Sara is reading a book. I am writing this answer.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Present Progressive","Different subjects (he/she/I)"],"explanation":"is/am/are + verb-ing for ongoing actions."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"What is the simple past of 'play'?","options":["played","playing","plays","play"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"Regular verb: play → played (-ed)."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose the correct form: 'Yesterday I ___ to the cinema.'","options":["go","goes","went","going"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"'Go' is irregular: go → went."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose the correct negation: 'She ___ come yesterday.'","options":["didn't","doesn't","wasn't","isn't"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"Past negation uses 'didn't' + base verb."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"What is the simple past of 'see'?","options":["seed","saw","seen","sawed"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Irregular: see → saw → seen."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose the correct question form: '___ you finish your homework?'","options":["Did","Do","Were","Was"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"Past question with 'do' becomes 'Did + subject + base verb'."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'My dog ___ very loudly last night.'","options":["barked","barks","bark","barking"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"Regular verb: bark → barked (-ed)."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which is the past form of 'have'?","options":["haved","had","haved","has"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Irregular: have → had."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'They ___ a great party last weekend.'","options":["have","had","haved","has"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Simple past of 'have': had."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'I ___ my keys yesterday and I'm still looking for them.'","options":["lost","loosed","losed","loose"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"Irregular: lose → lost."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"Yesterday I ___ a great movie. (watch)","correctAnswer":"watched","hint":"Regular -ed","explanation":"'Watched' — regular verb + -ed."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"She ___ to school yesterday. (go)","correctAnswer":"went","hint":"Irregular: go → ?","explanation":"'Went' — irregular past of 'go'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"They ___ a cake for the party. (make)","correctAnswer":"made","hint":"Irregular: make → ?","explanation":"'Made' — irregular past of 'make'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"I ___ not sleep well last night. (do/did)","correctAnswer":"did","hint":"Past negation","explanation":"'Did not sleep' = past negation with 'did'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"Where ___ you go on holiday last summer? (do/did)","correctAnswer":"did","hint":"Past question","explanation":"'Did you go' — past question form."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"My grandfather ___ this story when I was little. (tell)","correctAnswer":"told","hint":"Irregular: tell → ?","explanation":"'Told' — irregular past of 'tell'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"We ___ very tired after the long walk. (be)","correctAnswer":"were","hint":"Plural past of 'be'","explanation":"'Were' — past plural of 'be'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"She ___ a beautiful song at the concert. (sing)","correctAnswer":"sang","hint":"Irregular: sing → ?","explanation":"'Sang' — irregular past of 'sing'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"I ___ him in the park last Sunday. (meet)","correctAnswer":"met","hint":"Irregular: meet → ?","explanation":"'Met' — irregular past of 'meet'."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Regular verbs add -ed in the past tense.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: play → played, watch → watched."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Past negation uses 'didn't' followed by the past form of the verb.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — 'didn't' + BASE verb: 'didn't go' (not 'didn't went')."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Was' is the past form of 'is' (singular).","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: I/he/she/it was; we/you/they were."}

{"type":"true_false","statement":"To ask a question in past tense, use 'Did' + subject + past form.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — use 'Did' + subject + BASE form: 'Did you go?' (not 'Did you went?')."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"All English verbs become regular in the past tense.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — many common verbs are irregular: go/went, see/saw, have/had."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'I have done my homework' is Simple Past.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — that's Present Perfect. Simple Past is 'I did my homework'."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order (negation).","scrambledItems":["not","I","do","yesterday","go","did"],"correctOrder":["I","did","not","go","yesterday"],"explanation":"Subject + did + not + base verb + time."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order (question).","scrambledItems":["Did","you","yesterday","what","do","?"],"correctOrder":["What","did","you","do","yesterday","?"],"explanation":"Question word + did + subject + base verb + time."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["concert","sang","beautiful","song","She","at","a","the"],"correctOrder":["She","sang","a","beautiful","song","at","the","concert"],"explanation":"Subject + Verb + Object + Place."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["very","were","tired","after","walk","the","We"],"correctOrder":["We","were","very","tired","after","the","walk"],"explanation":"Subject + were + adjective + time/cause clause."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three sentences about what you did yesterday. Use simple past.","sampleAnswer":"Yesterday I went to school. After school I played with my friends. In the evening I watched TV.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Simple past forms","Past time markers (yesterday, after school, in the evening)"],"explanation":"Use past forms (went, played, watched) with time markers."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Tell about your last weekend in 3 sentences (simple past).","sampleAnswer":"Last weekend I visited my grandparents. We had lunch together. Then we played games in the garden.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Simple past","Mix of regular and irregular verbs"],"explanation":"Verbs: visited (regular), had (irregular), played (regular)."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three sentences using both regular AND irregular past verbs.","sampleAnswer":"I walked to school in the morning. I saw my friend on the way. We talked about the test.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Mix of regular (walked, talked) and irregular (saw)","Past tense"],"explanation":"Regular verbs add -ed; irregular verbs change form."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Tell a short story (3 sentences) about something funny that happened to you (simple past).","sampleAnswer":"Last week my dog ate my homework. I told my teacher the truth. She didn't believe me at first.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Simple past","Funny event"],"explanation":"Use past forms naturally to tell a short story."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write a short paragraph (3-4 sentences) about your last birthday. Include at least one negative sentence with 'didn't'.","sampleAnswer":"My last birthday was wonderful. I got many gifts and ate a chocolate cake. We invited my whole class, but my best friend didn't come because she was sick. I missed her, but the party was still fun.","keyPoints":["3-4 sentences","Includes a 'didn't' negation","Past forms throughout"],"explanation":"'didn't' + base verb (didn't come)."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write 3 sentences using these irregular past forms: went, saw, had.","sampleAnswer":"Last summer my family went to Spain. We saw beautiful beaches there. We had so much fun every day.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Each with one of: went, saw, had","Logical connection"],"explanation":"All three are common irregular past forms — important to remember."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which is a subject pronoun?","options":["me","I","my","mine"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"'I' is the subject pronoun. 'me' = object, 'my' = possessive adjective."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'This book is ___ .' (belongs to me)","options":["my","mine","I","me"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"After 'be': use possessive pronoun 'mine'."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'Can you help ___ ?'","options":["I","me","my","mine"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"After verb: object pronoun 'me'."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: '___ are my friends.'","options":["Them","Their","They","Theirs"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"Subject of sentence: 'They'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'This pen isn't mine. It's ___ .' (belongs to her)","options":["her","hers","she","herself"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"After 'be' + possession: 'hers' (possessive pronoun)."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'The teacher gave ___ a present.' (to us)","options":["we","our","us","ours"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"After verb: object pronoun 'us'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ am a student. (I/me)","correctAnswer":"I","hint":"Subject — capital letter","explanation":"Subject pronoun: 'I' (always capitalized)."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"This is ___ book. (my/mine)","correctAnswer":"my","hint":"Before noun = adjective","explanation":"'My book' — possessive adjective before noun."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"She called ___ yesterday. (I/me)","correctAnswer":"me","hint":"Object after verb","explanation":"After verb: object pronoun 'me'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"The cat licks ___ paws. (its/it's)","correctAnswer":"its","hint":"Possessive (no apostrophe)","explanation":"'Its' = possessive; 'it's' = it is."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"Those bikes are ___ . (they/their/theirs)","correctAnswer":"theirs","hint":"After 'be' + possession","explanation":"Possessive pronoun after 'be': 'theirs'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ teacher is very nice. (we/our)","correctAnswer":"Our","hint":"Possessive adjective before noun","explanation":"'Our teacher' — possessive adjective."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'I' is the only English word that is always capitalized.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct — the subject pronoun 'I' is always capitalized."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Me' is a subject pronoun.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — 'me' is an object pronoun. The subject is 'I'."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'My' goes before a noun (my book), 'mine' stands alone (this is mine).","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: 'my' = adjective; 'mine' = pronoun."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'It's' and 'its' mean the same thing.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — 'it's' = it is; 'its' = belongs to it. Different meanings!"}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"After a preposition, we use object pronouns (with me, for him).","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: with me, for him, to her, with us."}

{"type":"true_false","statement":"The possessive pronoun 'his' has the same form as the possessive adjective 'his'.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct — 'his' is the same in both: 'his book' (adj) and 'this is his' (pronoun)."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["a","I","student","am"],"correctOrder":["I","am","a","student"],"explanation":"Subject + verb + article + noun."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["is","name","my","Tim"],"correctOrder":["My","name","is","Tim"],"explanation":"Possessive adjective + noun + verb + name."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["help","you","me","Can","?"],"correctOrder":["Can","you","help","me","?"],"explanation":"Question with object pronoun 'me'."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["our","is","teacher","kind","very"],"correctOrder":["Our","teacher","is","very","kind"],"explanation":"Possessive + noun + be + adverb + adjective."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["are","Those","theirs","books"],"correctOrder":["Those","books","are","theirs"],"explanation":"Demonstrative + plural noun + are + possessive pronoun."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["him","gave","her","She","present","a"],"correctOrder":["She","gave","him","a","present"],"explanation":"Subject + verb + indirect object (him) + direct object."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three sentences using 'I', 'you', and 'we' as subjects.","sampleAnswer":"I like music. You are my friend. We play together.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Each uses I, you, we","Subject position"],"explanation":"Subject pronouns: I, you, we."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write two sentences with possessive adjectives (my, your, his).","sampleAnswer":"My dog is brown. Your bag is heavy.","keyPoints":["Two sentences","Possessive adjectives before nouns"],"explanation":"Possessive adjectives go before nouns: my dog, your bag, his book."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three sentences using subject AND object pronouns (e.g., I see her, He likes me).","sampleAnswer":"I see her every day. He likes me. We help them.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Both subject and object pronouns","Correct positions"],"explanation":"Subject before verb (I, he, we), object after (her, me, them)."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Describe your family in 3 sentences using possessive adjectives.","sampleAnswer":"My father is tall. My mother loves cooking. My sister and I share our room.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Possessive adjectives","Family vocabulary"],"explanation":"Use my, our + family nouns."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three sentences each with a different possessive pronoun (mine, yours, theirs).","sampleAnswer":"This pen is mine. That bag is yours. The new bikes are theirs.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Each with a different possessive pronoun","After 'be'"],"explanation":"Possessive pronouns stand alone after 'be': mine, yours, hers, his, ours, theirs."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write a short text (3 sentences) about helping someone — use object pronouns.","sampleAnswer":"My friend asked me for help. I helped her with her homework. She thanked us afterwards.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Object pronouns (me, her, us)","Logical context"],"explanation":"Object pronouns after verbs and prepositions."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"How do you ask 'are you happy' in correct English?","options":["You are happy?","Are you happy?","Happy you are?","Are happy you?"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Yes/no question: Verb (are) + Subject (you) + ..."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose the correct question word: '___ is your name?'","options":["Where","What","When","Why"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Asking for a name → 'What'."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: '___ do you live?'","options":["What","Where","When","Who"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Asking for a place → 'Where'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: '___ does she like ice cream? — Because it's sweet.'","options":["Why","What","Where","When"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"Asking for a reason → 'Why' (answered with 'Because')."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: '___ you like pizza?'","options":["Are","Is","Do","Does"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"With 'you' + base verb: 'Do you like'."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: '___ Lisa go to school?'","options":["Do","Does","Is","Are"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"3rd person singular: 'Does Lisa go'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ is your favorite color? (which question word?)","correctAnswer":"What","hint":"Capital letter at start","explanation":"'What' for things."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ are you? (asking about location)","correctAnswer":"Where","hint":"Place","explanation":"'Where' for location."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ do you go to bed? (time)","correctAnswer":"When","hint":"Time","explanation":"'When' for time."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ is your teacher? (person)","correctAnswer":"Who","hint":"Person","explanation":"'Who' for people."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ you like ice cream? (yes/no question)","correctAnswer":"Do","hint":"Auxiliary at start","explanation":"'Do' for yes/no questions with 'you'."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ she play tennis? (yes/no question)","correctAnswer":"Does","hint":"3rd person sg auxiliary","explanation":"'Does' for 3rd person singular yes/no questions."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"In yes/no questions, the verb comes before the subject.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: 'Are you ready?' — verb (are) + subject (you)."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Why' asks about time.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — 'why' asks about a reason. 'When' asks about time."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"With 'do', the main verb stays in its base form.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: 'Do you LIKE...' (not 'Do you LIKES')."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"In wh-questions, the question word comes at the end.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — wh-questions start with the question word: 'Where do you live?'"}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"For 3rd person singular questions, we use 'Does' + base verb.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: 'Does she play?' (not 'Does she plays?')."}

{"type":"true_false","statement":"'How' can be combined with other words like 'how much' or 'how often'.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: how much (price/amount), how often (frequency), how old (age)."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order to form a question.","scrambledItems":["?","you","Are","ready"],"correctOrder":["Are","you","ready","?"],"explanation":"Verb + subject + adjective + ?"}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["?","name","is","What","your"],"correctOrder":["What","is","your","name","?"],"explanation":"Question word + verb + possessive + noun + ?"}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["?","you","do","live","Where"],"correctOrder":["Where","do","you","live","?"],"explanation":"Wh-word + auxiliary + subject + verb + ?"}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["does","?","Sarah","do","What"],"correctOrder":["What","does","Sarah","do","?"],"explanation":"Wh-word + does (3rd sg) + subject + base verb + ?"}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["pizza","?","Why","you","like","do"],"correctOrder":["Why","do","you","like","pizza","?"],"explanation":"Wh-word + auxiliary + subject + verb + object + ?"}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["come","does","to","train","when","?","The"],"correctOrder":["When","does","the","train","come","?"],"explanation":"When + does + subject + base verb + ?"}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three questions you could ask a new friend.","sampleAnswer":"What is your name? Where do you live? How old are you?","keyPoints":["Three questions","Question marks","Different question words"],"explanation":"Common starter questions: name, place, age."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write two yes/no questions starting with 'Do' or 'Are'.","sampleAnswer":"Do you like cats? Are you tired?","keyPoints":["Two questions","Yes/no format","Starts with Do or Are"],"explanation":"Yes/no: Auxiliary first (Do/Are/Is/Does)."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three questions with 'What', 'Where', and 'When'.","sampleAnswer":"What do you eat for breakfast? Where do you go to school? When do you do your homework?","keyPoints":["Three questions","Each with a different wh-word","Auxiliary 'do' included"],"explanation":"Wh-word + do + subject + base verb."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Imagine a friend just came back from a trip. Write 3 questions to ask them.","sampleAnswer":"Where did you go? What did you see? Did you have fun?","keyPoints":["Three questions","Past tense (did)","Logical for a trip"],"explanation":"Past questions use 'did' + base verb."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three different types of questions: a yes/no, a wh-, and one with 'how'.","sampleAnswer":"Do you like math? What is your favorite book? How often do you read?","keyPoints":["Three different question types","Yes/no, wh-, how-","Question marks"],"explanation":"Variety in question types makes for richer language."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write three questions you could ask a teacher about your homework.","sampleAnswer":"When is the homework due? What pages do we need to read? Can I get help if I need it?","keyPoints":["Three questions","About homework","Polite tone"],"explanation":"School-related questions about deadlines, content, help."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose the comparative: 'My brother is ___ than me.' (tall)","options":["tall","taller","tallest","more tall"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Short adjective + -er: 'taller'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose the superlative: 'This is the ___ book.' (good)","options":["gooder","best","more good","goodest"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"'Good' is irregular: good — better — best."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'A car is ___ than a bike.' (fast)","options":["faster","more fast","fastest","fasterer"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"Short adjective + -er: 'faster'."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'This is the ___ test of all.' (difficult)","options":["difficulter","most difficult","more difficult","difficultest"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Long adjective: 'most difficult' (superlative)."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'Today is ___ than yesterday.' (bad)","options":["badder","baddest","worse","worst"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"'Bad' is irregular: bad — worse — worst."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Choose: 'Math is ___ for me than English.' (interesting)","options":["interestinger","more interesting","most interesting","interestingest"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Long adjective: 'more interesting' (comparative)."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"My sister is ___ than me. (young)","correctAnswer":"younger","hint":"+ -er","explanation":"'Younger' — short adjective + -er."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"This is the ___ cake! (delicious — superlative)","correctAnswer":"most delicious","hint":"long adjective: most + ...","explanation":"'Most delicious' — long adjective superlative."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"An elephant is ___ than a dog. (big)","correctAnswer":"bigger","hint":"Double the consonant + -er","explanation":"'Bigger' — short adj ending in vowel+consonant doubles the consonant."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"This is the ___ river in the world. (long)","correctAnswer":"longest","hint":"+ -est","explanation":"'Longest' — short adjective + -est."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"My grades are ___ than last year. (good)","correctAnswer":"better","hint":"Irregular comparative","explanation":"'Better' — irregular: good → better → best."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"He is the ___ runner in the team. (fast)","correctAnswer":"fastest","hint":"+ -est","explanation":"'Fastest' — short adjective superlative."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Short adjectives form the comparative with '+er' (e.g., tall → taller).","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: tall → taller, fast → faster, big → bigger."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Good' becomes 'gooder' in the comparative.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — 'good' is irregular: good → better → best."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Long adjectives use 'more' (comparative) and 'most' (superlative).","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: difficult → more difficult → most difficult."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"In the superlative, you usually need 'the' before the adjective.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: 'the tallest', 'the best', 'the most beautiful'."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"For adjectives ending in -y (like 'happy'), we change y to i and add -er or -est.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: happy → happier → happiest."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Bad becomes 'badder' in the comparative form.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — 'bad' is irregular: bad → worse → worst."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["taller","is","brother","my","than","I"],"correctOrder":["My","brother","is","taller","than","I"],"explanation":"Subject + be + comparative + 'than' + comparison."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["best","This","is","book","the"],"correctOrder":["This","is","the","best","book"],"explanation":"Subject + be + 'the' + superlative + noun."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["bigger","than","An","elephant","is","dog","a"],"correctOrder":["An","elephant","is","bigger","than","a","dog"],"explanation":"Subject + be + comparative + than + noun."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["interesting","more","Math","English","is","than"],"correctOrder":["Math","is","more","interesting","than","English"],"explanation":"Subject + be + 'more' + adjective + 'than' + noun."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["happiest","She","of","is","the","all","us"],"correctOrder":["She","is","the","happiest","of","all","us"],"explanation":"Superlative pattern: ... is the ... -est of all."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the words in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["most","film","beautiful","the","I","seen","ever","have"],"correctOrder":["The","most","beautiful","film","I","have","ever","seen"],"explanation":"Superlative + noun + relative clause."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Compare yourself with a friend. Write 2 sentences using 'taller', 'older', or 'younger'.","sampleAnswer":"My friend Tom is taller than me. I am older than my sister.","keyPoints":["Two sentences","Comparative forms","'than' included"],"explanation":"Comparative: ... is + adj-er + than ..."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write two sentences with superlatives (e.g., 'the best', 'the tallest').","sampleAnswer":"Math is the best subject. Mount Everest is the tallest mountain.","keyPoints":["Two sentences","Superlative forms","'the' included"],"explanation":"Superlative: the + ...-est OR the most + adj."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Compare two countries you know in 3 sentences (use comparatives).","sampleAnswer":"Germany is bigger than Switzerland. Switzerland has more mountains than Germany. Germany is more famous for cars.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Comparative forms","Comparison between two things"],"explanation":"Use both -er and 'more' comparatives appropriately."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Describe your favorite person — write 3 sentences with adjectives in different forms (positive, comparative, superlative).","sampleAnswer":"My grandmother is kind. She is kinder than most people I know. She is the kindest person in my family.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Three forms (positive, comparative, superlative)","Same adjective family"],"explanation":"kind → kinder → kindest."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write 3 sentences with irregular comparatives/superlatives (good/better/best, bad/worse/worst).","sampleAnswer":"This pizza is better than the one we had last week. My grades are better in math than in English. The worst day of my life was when I broke my leg.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Irregular forms","Logical context"],"explanation":"good → better → best; bad → worse → worst."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Compare three of your favorite hobbies in a short text (3 sentences).","sampleAnswer":"Reading is more relaxing than watching TV. But playing football is more fun than reading. Of all my hobbies, drawing is the most creative.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Three hobbies compared","Mix of comparative and superlative"],"explanation":"Use 'more' for long adjectives, 'most' for superlative."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which spelling is correct?","options":["frend","friend","freind","frind"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"'Friend' — remember 'i before e except after c'."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which spelling is correct?","options":["beautifull","beatiful","beautiful","butiful"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"'Beautiful' — bea-u-ti-ful."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which spelling is correct?","options":["recieve","receive","receeve","recive"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"'Receive' — 'i before e EXCEPT after c'."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which spelling is correct?","options":["happines","happiness","hapyness","happynes"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"'Happiness' — happy + -ness, but y → i."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which spelling is correct?","options":["necesary","neccessary","necessary","necesarry"],"correctIndex":2,"explanation":"'Necessary' — one c, two s."}
{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which spelling is correct?","options":["bicycle","bycicle","bicicle","bisycle"],"correctIndex":0,"explanation":"'Bicycle' — bi + cycle."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"My best ___ is Tom. (a person you like)","correctAnswer":"friend","hint":"i before e","explanation":"'Friend' — silent 'i'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"This flower is very ___ . (pretty)","correctAnswer":"beautiful","hint":"bea-u-ti-ful","explanation":"'Beautiful' is often misspelled."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"The teacher gave us a ___ . (writing task)","correctAnswer":"lesson","hint":"two s's","explanation":"'Lesson' has two s's."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"I went to the ___ to buy bread. (food shop)","correctAnswer":"supermarket","hint":"super + market","explanation":"'Supermarket' — compound word."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"It is ___ to brush your teeth. (must do)","correctAnswer":"necessary","hint":"one c, two s","explanation":"'Necessary' — common spelling trap."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"I want to get a ___ for my birthday. (two-wheeled vehicle)","correctAnswer":"bicycle","hint":"bi + cycle","explanation":"'Bicycle' — bi (two) + cycle (wheel)."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Friend' is spelled with the letters 'i e'.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: F-R-I-E-N-D."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Beautiful' is spelled with two 'u's.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — only one 'u': bea-u-ti-ful."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"The rule 'i before e except after c' helps with words like 'receive'.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: re-ceive (after c → 'ei')."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Tomorrow' has one 'm' and two 'r's.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: to-mor-row (1 m, 2 r)."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"'Necessary' has two c's.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — 'necessary' has one c and two s's."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Words ending in -tion are usually nouns (e.g., action, station).","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: -tion is a common noun ending."}

Why did they put on rain boots?","options":["because they were sad","because it was raining","because they were hungry","because they were tired"],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Inference: rain → rain boots."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"Text: 'Tina is nine years old.' Question: How old is Tina? — ___ years old.","correctAnswer":"nine","explanation":"Direct from text."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"Text: 'In summer, we go to the sea and swim every day.' Question: When do they go to the sea? — In ___","correctAnswer":"summer","explanation":"Text: 'In summer'."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"Text: 'Jens has two siblings, a brother and a sister.' Question: How many siblings? — ___","correctAnswer":"two","explanation":"Text: 'two siblings'."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Text: 'Mia loves reading books.' Statement: Mia likes books.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"'Loves reading' = likes books → true."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Text: 'It is Monday, 7 a.m.' Statement: It is evening.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"7 a.m. = morning, not evening."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Text: 'All the children were hungry and looked forward to lunch.' Statement: The children were full.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"'Hungry' = opposite of 'full'."}

{"type":"true_false","statement":"Text: 'Jana doesn't like piano, but she practices an hour every day.' Statement: Jana practices piano daily.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"'Practices every day' = daily, even though she dislikes it."}

{"type":"true_false","statement":"Text: 'Tim wanted to go to the cinema, but the film was sold out, so he went to the swimming pool instead.' Statement: Tim watched a film at the cinema.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"Sold out → went to pool instead, no film."}

{"type":"true_false","statement":"Text: 'Although it was snowing, the bus was on time.' Statement: Snow delayed the bus.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"'Although' shows contrast: snow did NOT delay the bus."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences in the correct order (small story).","scrambledItems":["Lina goes to school.","She packs her schoolbag.","She gets up early."],"correctOrder":["She gets up early.","She packs her schoolbag.","Lina goes to school."],"explanation":"Logical order: get up → pack → go."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["She eats it.","Anna buys a banana.","Anna is hungry."],"correctOrder":["Anna is hungry.","Anna buys a banana.","She eats it."],"explanation":"Hungry → buy → eat."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["We played at the beach.","In summer we drove to the sea.","In the evening we went to bed early.","We were tired."],"correctOrder":["In summer we drove to the sea.","We played at the beach.","We were tired.","In the evening we went to bed early."],"explanation":"Time sequence: travel → activity → consequence → evening."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["They planted an apple tree.","In spring the kids were in the garden.","In autumn they could pick apples.","The tree grew quickly."],"correctOrder":["In spring the kids were in the garden.","They planted an apple tree.","The tree grew quickly.","In autumn they could pick apples."],"explanation":"Spring → plant → grow → autumn → harvest."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["Suddenly it started raining.","They took shelter under a big tree.","The children were playing in the park.","After an hour, the rain stopped."],"correctOrder":["The children were playing in the park.","Suddenly it started raining.","They took shelter under a big tree.","After an hour, the rain stopped."],"explanation":"Setting → event → reaction → resolution."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences in the correct order.","scrambledItems":["He got his tools.","Paul's bike had a flat tire.","Within minutes, everything was fixed.","Patiently he repaired the hole."],"correctOrder":["Paul's bike had a flat tire.","He got his tools.","Patiently he repaired the hole.","Within minutes, everything was fixed."],"explanation":"Problem → preparation → action → solution."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Read: 'Today we go to the zoo with our class. We see elephants, giraffes, and lions.' What do the children see at the zoo? Answer in one sentence.","sampleAnswer":"At the zoo, the children see elephants, giraffes, and lions.","keyPoints":["Full sentence","Names the animals","Correct spelling"],"explanation":"Direct answer from the text."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Read: 'Lukas plays football every Wednesday with his friends in the park.' When and where does Lukas play football? Answer in one sentence.","sampleAnswer":"Lukas plays football every Wednesday in the park.","keyPoints":["Full sentence","Mentions Wednesday and park","Answers the question"],"explanation":"When = every Wednesday, where = in the park."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Read: 'Lara had a birthday. Her friends brought many gifts and sang a song. Lara was very happy.' What did Laras friends do, and how did Lara feel? Answer in 2 sentences.","sampleAnswer":"Laras friends brought gifts and sang a song. Lara was very happy.","keyPoints":["Two sentences","What the friends did","How Lara felt"],"explanation":"Friends: gifts + song. Lara: happy."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Read: 'It was cold, but Felix wanted to eat ice cream. His mother said: 'In winter, that's a strange idea.' Felix laughed and bought one anyway.' Why was the mother surprised? Answer in 1-2 sentences.","sampleAnswer":"The mother was surprised because Felix wanted ice cream in winter, when it is cold.","keyPoints":["Answers the question","Explains the reason","Reference to text"],"explanation":"Inference: ice cream + cold weather = unusual."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Read: 'Sophie had promised to help her sister. But then her best friend called and invited her to the cinema. Sophie thought for a long time about what to do.' What was Sophie's problem? Answer in 2-3 sentences.","sampleAnswer":"Sophie had two options: help her sister or go to the cinema with her friend. She had promised to help her sister, but the cinema invitation was tempting. So she could not decide what to do.","keyPoints":["2-3 sentences","Explains Sophie's conflict","Reference to text"],"explanation":"Conflict between promise and invitation."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Read: 'Despite the rain, the children went on the trip. They got wet, but they had a lot of fun.' How was the trip in the end? Answer in 1-2 sentences.","sampleAnswer":"The trip was nice, even though the children got wet. They still had a lot of fun.","keyPoints":["Answers the question","Uses 'although' or 'even though'","Reference to text"],"explanation":"Despite rain → still had fun."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which is a good opening sentence for an introduction?","options":["I am.","Hi, my name is Marc and I am 10 years old.","Things.","The end."],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"A good intro names yourself and gives basic info."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which sentence describes a hobby clearly?","options":["I do things.","I like soccer because I play it every weekend with my friends.","Hobby.","Yes."],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"A clear sentence names the hobby and adds detail."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which is a more interesting way to describe a day?","options":["My day was good.","I started my day with a sunny walk in the park before breakfast.","Day.","Yes I had a day."],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Specific details make writing interesting."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which is a good way to end a short text?","options":["The end.","To sum up, my favorite hobby helps me relax and have fun.","Bye.","I stop."],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"A summary or conclusion brings the text to a clear ending."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which connector best joins two related ideas?","options":["But","And","However","All of the above can connect ideas"],"correctIndex":3,"explanation":"And, but, however — all are connectors. Choose based on the relationship."}

{"type":"multiple_choice","question":"Which sentence shows good use of an adjective?","options":["The dog runs.","The big black dog runs quickly across the field.","Dog runs.","Yes dog."],"correctIndex":1,"explanation":"Adjectives (big, black) and adverbs (quickly) make sentences vivid."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"___ name is Marc. (introduction)","correctAnswer":"My","hint":"Possessive adjective","explanation":"'My name is...' — common introduction."}

{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"My favorite hobby is ___ . (something fun you do)","correctAnswer":"reading","acceptableAnswers":["football","drawing","music","swimming","gaming"],"hint":"name a hobby","explanation":"Common hobbies: reading, football, drawing, etc."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"In the morning ___ go to school. (subject pronoun)","correctAnswer":"I","hint":"capital letter","explanation":"'I' (always capitalized)."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"My favorite subject is ___ ___ I like numbers. (connector)","correctAnswer":"because","hint":"reason","explanation":"'Because' gives a reason."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"I like both football ___ basketball. (connector for similarity)","correctAnswer":"and","hint":"connector","explanation":"'And' connects similar ideas."}
{"type":"fill_in_the_blank","sentence":"I love summer, ___ I don't like the heat. (contrast)","correctAnswer":"but","hint":"opposite/contrast","explanation":"'But' shows contrast."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"A good text always has at least 3 sentences.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Yes, even short writing should have a beginning, middle, and end."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"You can start every sentence with 'I'.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"It's better to vary sentence beginnings: 'In the morning I...', 'My family...', etc."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"Adjectives make writing more colorful and interesting.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Correct: adjectives like 'big', 'happy', 'beautiful' add detail."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"It's good to use connectors like 'and', 'but', 'because'.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Yes — connectors make writing flow better."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"In a short text, every sentence should be very long.","isTrue":false,"explanation":"False — mix short and long sentences for variety."}
{"type":"true_false","statement":"You should re-read your writing and check for spelling.","isTrue":true,"explanation":"Always proofread for spelling and grammar mistakes."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences of a short introduction in order.","scrambledItems":["I love football and reading.","I am ten years old.","Hi, my name is Marc."],"correctOrder":["Hi, my name is Marc.","I am ten years old.","I love football and reading."],"explanation":"Greeting → basic info → details."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences of a short text about your day in order.","scrambledItems":["At noon I have lunch.","In the evening I read.","In the morning I go to school."],"correctOrder":["In the morning I go to school.","At noon I have lunch.","In the evening I read."],"explanation":"Time order: morning → noon → evening."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences of a hobby description in order.","scrambledItems":["I have been doing this since I was 7.","I love it because it is fun and creative.","My favorite hobby is drawing."],"correctOrder":["My favorite hobby is drawing.","I have been doing this since I was 7.","I love it because it is fun and creative."],"explanation":"Topic → background → reason."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences in order.","scrambledItems":["I usually have soup for dinner.","Then I read a book.","After school I do my homework."],"correctOrder":["After school I do my homework.","I usually have soup for dinner.","Then I read a book."],"explanation":"Time order: after school → dinner → reading."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences of a paragraph in order.","scrambledItems":["I want to learn many new things there.","Although the new school is far, I am excited.","Next year I will go to a new school.","I will also make new friends."],"correctOrder":["Next year I will go to a new school.","Although the new school is far, I am excited.","I want to learn many new things there.","I will also make new friends."],"explanation":"Topic → contrast → goals → expectations."}

{"type":"reorder","instruction":"Put the sentences of an introduction in order.","scrambledItems":["I have a younger brother and a cat named Luna.","My family lives in Munich.","Hello, I am Sara, and I am eleven years old.","We often go hiking together."],"correctOrder":["Hello, I am Sara, and I am eleven years old.","My family lives in Munich.","I have a younger brother and a cat named Luna.","We often go hiking together."],"explanation":"Greeting → family → details → activity."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Introduce yourself in 3 sentences.","sampleAnswer":"Hi, my name is Marc. I am ten years old. I love playing football with my friends.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Self-introduction","Includes name and one detail"],"explanation":"Basic introduction: name + age + hobby/interest."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Describe your day in 3 sentences.","sampleAnswer":"In the morning I get up at 7. I go to school and have lots of fun. In the evening I play with my dog.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Time markers (morning, evening)","Daily activities"],"explanation":"Time markers help structure: morning, afternoon, evening."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write 3 sentences about your favorite hobby.","sampleAnswer":"My favorite hobby is reading books. I read every evening for an hour. I love adventure stories the most.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Names hobby","Adds detail"],"explanation":"Hobby + frequency + preference."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write a short text (3 sentences) about your best friend.","sampleAnswer":"My best friend is Tom. We have been friends since first grade. We always play football together after school.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","About a friend","Includes details about friendship"],"explanation":"Name + how/since when + what you do together."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write a short paragraph (3 sentences) about your family.","sampleAnswer":"My family is small. I have one brother and we live with our parents. We love going on weekend trips together.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","About family","Use of 'we' or family members"],"explanation":"Family description + activities together."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Describe your room in 3 sentences.","sampleAnswer":"My room is not very big but it is cozy. There is a desk by the window and a bookshelf full of books. I love spending time there.","keyPoints":["Three sentences","Describes the room","Uses 'there is/are'"],"explanation":"Use 'there is/are' for describing places."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write a short text (3-4 sentences) about your dream day. Use connectors (and, but, because).","sampleAnswer":"My dream day would start with a delicious breakfast in bed. After that, I would go to a theme park with my friends because I love rollercoasters. We would eat ice cream and laugh all day. In the evening, I would relax with a good movie.","keyPoints":["3-4 sentences","Uses connectors","Logical flow"],"explanation":"Connectors: and (similar), but (contrast), because (reason)."}

{"type":"free_text","prompt":"Write a short paragraph (3-4 sentences) about your favorite season.","sampleAnswer":"My favorite season is summer. I love it because the weather is warm and sunny. We can swim, play outside, and have ice cream. The best part is the long summer holidays.","keyPoints":["3-4 sentences","Names season","Gives reasons"],"explanation":"Topic + reason + activities + summary."}
